name: Adopt include_dir + establish crates/ws-cli/assets/ as canonical asset location (ADR Q3)
description:
## What to build

End-to-end: the Cargo workspace gains the include_dir crate as a dependency of ws-cli, the directory crates/ws-cli/assets/ exists as the single canonical embedded-asset location (per restructure ADR Decision 3), and an include_dir!("$CARGO_MANIFEST_DIR/assets") macro is wired so its Dir::entries() iterator is usable by future scaffold loops. This is the restructure-level infrastructure — broader than the ws kb feature — and the first consumer (the ws kb epic) depends on it landing.

## Context

Resolves open question Q3 on docs/cli-centric-restructure-plan.md. The restructure ADR recommends include_dir! over rust-embed (heavier; no MIME/compression needs) and manual include_str! per file (high drift risk — the exact problem embedding is killing). The include_dir Dir::entries() iterator maps 1:1 onto a recursive fs::write scaffold loop, so the same embedded tree powers both runtime reads (Tier 1) and ws init scaffolding (Tier 3).

This ticket does NOT move any specific asset subtree (the ws kb epic moves catalog/knowledge/ into assets/catalog-knowledge/ in its own ticket). It only stands up the mechanism + the canonical directory.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] include_dir crate added as a dependency of ws-cli (workspace resolved, cargo build -p ws-cli green).
- [ ] crates/ws-cli/assets/ directory created (with a .gitkeep or first real subtree so it is tracked).
- [ ] include_dir!("$CARGO_MANIFEST_DIR/assets") wired and reachable from ws-cli code; a smoke check that the embedded Dir iterates (e.g. a tiny test or a one-line assertion in main) confirms assets are embedded at build time.
- [ ] No Tier-1/2/3 assets moved in this ticket — mechanism only. Subsequent features (ws kb, Tier 1 workflows/providers, Tier 2 schema generate) drop their assets into this dir and build on the macro.
- [ ] ADR §6 status updated to mark Q3 fully landed once the mechanism ships (Q3 is already marked RESOLVED at the decision level; this ticket is the implementation of that resolution).

## Blocked by

None — can start immediately. This is the gate for the ws kb epic's crate-skeleton ticket (wdvr8o03).
result:
PR #2 merged (merge commit fc33bda on main). include_dir mechanism landed per the new one-PR-per-ticket workflow.

Verification on merge:
- origin/main at fc33bda (merge commit), contains 8b80d24 (ADR Q3-Q6) + 9ecce75 (include_dir wiring) + 5bab7a7 (Cargo.lock).
- crates/ws-cli/assets/.gitkeep tracked and present on main; crates/ws-cli/src/assets.rs present.
- Local main fast-forwarded to fc33bda; merged feature branch deleted locally and on remote.
- Unblocks wdvr8o03 (ws-kb crate skeleton) — now in the ready frontier.

commit.message: feat(ws-cli): adopt include_dir + establish crates/ws-cli/assets/ (ADR Q3)

name: ws kb — Knowledge Base (epic)
description:
Ship the ws kb subcommand namespace (init + lint) backed by embedded assets, closing the latent-bug that ws init does not scaffold catalog/knowledge/ and giving the harness a single agent-maintained knowledge base holding both product and engineering streams.

Parent dependency (EXTERNAL, not a ticket): the restructure ADR's open Q3 must confirm adoption of the include_dir crate as the embedding mechanism and crates/ws-cli/assets/ as the canonical asset location. Ticket 1 is blocked on that decision landing.

Concurrency: tickets 2, 3, 6, 7 become ready simultaneously once ticket 1 lands; work them in separate context windows. Frontier after Q3 + ticket 1: tickets 3, 6, 7 (and 2).

Source spec: docs/features/knowledge-base.md. Parent ADR: docs/cli-centric-restructure-plan.md.

name: ws-kb::lint() MVP (orphans + broken wikilinks) + ws kb lint CLI
description:
## What to build

End-to-end: running ws kb lint in a workspace reports orphan pages (no inbound [[wikilinks]]) and broken [[wikilinks]] (target slug resolves to no page), emitting a parseable one-finding-per-line report. Clean wiki exits 0; findings exit non-zero (exit-code semantics to be confirmed with user during impl). The lint() library function is the pure seam; the CLI is a thin wrapper.

## Implementation notes (from spec)

- lint(root: &Path) -> Result<Vec<LintFinding>> scans wiki/**/*.md, parses [[wikilinks]], returns orphans + broken-link findings.
- [[wikilink]] resolution rule: a link target resolves if the slug (filename without .md) exists ANYWHERE under wiki/, folder-agnostic — [[revenue]] resolves to wiki/topics/revenue.md or wiki/entities/revenue.md. Broken = no page with that slug exists. Orphan = a wiki page whose slug is never the target of any [[wikilink]] across the wiki.
- This is the MVP's only graph-traversal code and seeds the future ws kb graph command.
- MVP scope is deliberately minimal: NO frontmatter validation, NO pointer-staleness revalidation, NO contradiction detection (those are agent-judged per SCHEMA, deferred).
- Output: parseable report (page path + finding type + severity + message), one per line, suitable for agent/CI consumption.
- Exit code: spec is ambiguous (exit 0 with warnings vs non-zero on findings) — confirm with user; pick exit-non-zero-on-findings unless told otherwise.
- Follow the tempdir test pattern established by ticket 1.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] ws-kb::lint(root) returns Vec<LintFinding> over wiki/**/*.md.
- [ ] Orphan detection: a page whose slug is never a [[wikilink]] target is reported.
- [ ] Broken-link detection: a [[wikilink]] to a nonexistent slug is reported.
- [ ] Slug resolution is folder-agnostic across topics/entities/sources/synthesis.
- [ ] Clean wiki (all links resolve, no orphans) yields no findings.
- [ ] ws kb lint CLI prints one finding per line (path + type + severity + message); exit 0 if clean, non-zero on findings (or 0-with-warnings if user prefers — confirm).
- [ ] Tempdir fixtures cover orphan, broken link, clean wiki, cross-folder resolution.
- [ ] cargo test -p ws-kb passes.
result: Implemented ws-kb::lint() MVP. lint(root) scans wiki/**/*.md, parses [[wikilinks]], returns orphans + broken-link findings with folder-agnostic slug resolution. ws kb lint CLI prints one finding per line, exits non-zero on findings. Tempdir fixtures cover orphan, broken link, clean wiki, cross-folder resolution. cargo test -p ws-kb green. Merged via PR #7.

name: Scaffolded SCHEMA.md + _template.md content additions: streams, provenance, concept capture
description:
## What to build

End-to-end: the embedded SCHEMA.md inside crates/ws-cli/assets/catalog-knowledge/ gains the new contract sections (both-streams, source types, pointer vocabulary, concept capture), and _template.md gains the stream frontmatter field. After rebuild, ws kb init in a fresh workspace reproduces a SCHEMA that gives the agent a precise contract for capturing both product and engineering concepts with hybrid provenance. No CLI call needed — content edits to the embedded asset bytes (rebuild re-embeds).

## Implementation notes (from spec §Implementation Decisions / Schema changes)

Add to SCHEMA.md:
- Both streams live here: single wiki holds product + engineering knowledge; distinguished by frontmatter stream: product | engineering; no folder partition; cross-stream [[wikilinks]] are first-class.
- Source types: blob sources (materialized in raw/, get a source page, cited [[src-<slug>]]) vs pointer sources (addresses to things living elsewhere, cited inline as typed tokens, no source page by default).
- Pointer vocabulary (MVP): commit:<sha>, file:<path>#Lstart-Lend, issue:<id>, pr:<num>, web:<url>, conversation:<date> (last appears ONLY on conversation-snapshot source pages as provenance marker, never bare inline on a topic page).
- Typed-token stored form ([commit:abc123]) vs clickable render form ([\`abc123\`](https://github.com/org/repo/commit/abc123)); ws kb lint does not parse/validate typed tokens in MVP.
- Opt-in concept-keyed source pages for pointer clusters: wiki/sources/src-<concept>-evidence.md, version-controlled, stable semantic id (NOT date-keyed), tagged external where the page is a cluster of external pointers. No raw/sources/external/ folder.
- Conversation-snapshot resolution: snapshot blob in raw/snapshots/<date>-<topic>.md (verbatim definition + paraphrased narrative, consent-gated) -> source page wiki/sources/src-conv-<date>-<topic>.md -> cited [[src-conv-...]].
- Concept capture operation: agent-judged gap detection (concept mentioned-without-a-page or referenced-in-work-but-absent), gather draft definition + sources list, optional human enrichment at the gate, yes/no gate invokes the SCHEMA-defined Ingest workflow on consent.
- Consent gate scope: fires on (a) new-page creation and (b) contradictions to existing claims; routine refinement edits (adding a citation, extending a summary, fixing a link) are ungated and logged in log.md.
- Note that the mechanical subset of Lint is delegated to ws kb lint; the judgment subset stays the agent's.

Add to _template.md: a stream frontmatter field (product | engineering).

Preserve existing Ingest/Query/Update/Lint operations, noting Ingest/Query/Update are agent-performed markdown edits.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] SCHEMA.md in the embedded assets contains both-streams section; product + engineering distinguished by stream frontmatter, no folder partition.
- [ ] Source types section enumerates blob vs pointer + full pointer vocabulary.
- [ ] Typed-token stored form vs clickable render form documented.
- [ ] Concept-keyed source page convention documented (src-<concept>-evidence, stable semantic id, external tag) and the raw/sources/external/ folder explicitly rejected.
- [ ] Conversation-snapshot resolution documented end-to-end.
- [ ] Concept-capture operation documented with the yes/no gate and its scope (gate on new-page + contradictions; routine edits ungated + logged).
- [ ] _template.md carries the stream frontmatter field.
- [ ] ws kb init still embeds the updated assets byte-for-byte (covered by ticket 1 tests still passing).
result: Extended embedded SCHEMA.md with both-streams section, source types (blob vs pointer), full pointer vocabulary (commit/file/issue/pr/web/conversation), typed-token stored vs render forms, concept-keyed source page convention, conversation-snapshot resolution, and concept-capture operation with consent gate. Added stream frontmatter field to _template.md. ws kb init still embeds updated assets byte-for-byte. Merged via PR #6.

name: ws kb init --reset <asset> single-asset refresh
description:
## What to build

End-to-end: ws kb init --reset SCHEMA.md rewrites exactly the named embedded asset from the binary and leaves every other file untouched, so an upstream improvement to one seed can be adopted without nuking the rest of the wiki. An unknown asset name errors with a list of valid names.

## Implementation notes (from spec)

- AssetId enum over known asset names (e.g. SCHEMA.md, README.md, _template.md, etc. — enumerate the embedded tree's seed files).
- scaffold(root, Some(AssetId)) rewrites the named asset from the embedded bytes, leaves all others untouched.
- CLI: ws kb init --reset <asset> delegates to that.
- Unknown asset name -> error listing valid asset names (the full AssetId set).
- --reset only refreshes the named asset; never touches other content. Idempotency (ticket 3) is the default (no --reset); --reset is the explicit escape hatch.
- Tempdir tests: reset rewrites exactly one asset (content matches new embedded bytes) and touches nothing else; unknown-name errors with the valid-name list.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] AssetId enum enumerates all known embedded seed assets.
- [ ] scaffold(root, Some(AssetId)) rewrites only the named asset.
- [ ] All other files untouched (tempdir test asserts byte-for-byte unchanged).
- [ ] ws kb init --reset <asset> delegates correctly.
- [ ] Unknown asset name -> non-zero exit + message listing valid asset names.
- [ ] Tempdir tests cover: single-asset refresh, nothing-else-touched, unknown-asset error.
- [ ] cargo test -p ws-kb passes.

name: Integrate KB scaffold into ws init (full-init produces complete KB tree)
description:
## What to build

End-to-end: a fresh ws init in an empty directory produces a complete knowledge-base tree alongside the catalog dirs and templates it already writes. The harness's existing AGENTS.md injection (ws-providers telling the agent to 'read catalog/knowledge/SCHEMA.md before maintaining the wiki') now resolves to a real file in every fresh workspace — closing the restructure ADR §2.3 latent bug end-to-end. Re-running ws init on an existing workspace preserves accumulated wiki content (via ticket 3's skip semantics).

## Implementation notes (from spec)

- handle_init (crates/ws-cli/src/main.rs, ~line 361) currently writes catalog dirs + one template/svc yaml + sample product/team; it does NOT scaffold the knowledge tree. Add a ws-kb::scaffold(root, None) call as one of its module steps.
- Idempotency (ticket 3) makes this safe: existing wiki content is preserved, only gaps filled.
- ws kb init (ticket 2) remains available standalone for set-up/repair without a full ws init.
- Wire after the existing ws_catalog::ensure_catalog_dirs call (so catalog/knowledge/ parent exists), or let ws-kb::scaffold create it itself.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] handle_init invokes ws-kb::scaffold(root, None).
- [ ] Fresh ws init in a tempdir produces a real catalog/knowledge/SCHEMA.md (resolves the AGENTS.md injection that references it).
- [ ] Fresh ws init produces the full KB tree (raw/, wiki/index.md, log.md, _template.md, topic/entity/source/synthesis dirs).
- [ ] Re-running ws init on a workspace with user-edited wiki content preserves it (skip-by-default from ticket 3).
- [ ] cargo build -p ws-cli green; existing ws init behavior for catalog/templates unchanged.
- [ ] Manual sanity: ws init in a tempdir then check catalog/knowledge/SCHEMA.md exists.

name: ws-kb crate skeleton: embedded assets + scaffold() core + tests
description:
## What to build

End-to-end: a new ws-kb crate exists whose pure function scaffold(root: &Path, reset: Option<AssetId>) -> Result<ScaffoldReport> writes the complete knowledge-base tree from embedded canonical assets, and the first Rust test pattern in the workspace (tempfile::TempDir fixtures at the library seam) proves the tree is written byte-for-byte and re-runs correctly. No CLI yet — demoable via cargo test -p ws-kb.

## Blocked by

- 7p7r946y (Adopt include_dir + establish crates/ws-cli/assets/). That ticket lands the include_dir dependency and the canonical assets/ directory; this ticket moves catalog/knowledge/ into assets/catalog-knowledge/ and builds scaffold() on top.

## Implementation notes (from spec)

- Move the existing committed catalog/knowledge/ tree (SCHEMA.md, README.md, raw/ with README/.gitignore/assets/ .gitkeep, wiki/ with index.md, log.md, _template.md, topics/entities/sources/synthesis dirs .gitkeep'd) to crates/ws-cli/assets/catalog-knowledge/.
- Embed via include_dir (the macro is wired by ticket 7p7r946y). The Dir::entries() iterator maps 1:1 onto a recursive fs::write scaffold loop.
- scaffold() writes all embedded assets; ScaffoldReport records which assets were written/skipped/refreshed.
- This crate is the seam: two pure operations over a filesystem root. Match the idiom of ws-catalog's ensure_catalog_dirs / add_knowledge / get_knowledge (pure functions over a workspace root) but go further by establishing the tempdir test pattern they lack.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] New ws-kb crate added to the Cargo workspace; depends on include_dir (and tempfile dev-dependency).
- [ ] catalog/knowledge/ tree moved to crates/ws-cli/assets/catalog-knowledge/ and embedded at build time.
- [ ] scaffold(root, None) writes the full tree under <root>/catalog/knowledge/.
- [ ] ScaffoldReport accurately records per-asset written/skipped/refreshed status.
- [ ] Tempdir tests assert: full tree written; embedded file contents match canonical assets byte-for-byte.
- [ ] cargo test -p ws-kb passes; cargo build -p ws-cli still green.
- [ ] First test pattern documented enough for subsequent tickets (lint, idempotency) to follow it.
result:
PR #3 merged (merge commit 5ec65fd on main). ws-kb crate skeleton landed.

What shipped (commit a69d19e):
- New ws-kb crate: KB_ASSETS (include_dir! embedded from crates/ws-cli/assets/catalog-knowledge/), scaffold(root) -> Result<ScaffoldReport> (skip-by-default: Written/Skipped), ScaffoldReport/AssetReport/AssetStatus types.
- Moved catalog/knowledge/ -> crates/ws-cli/assets/catalog-knowledge/ via git mv (history preserved).
- Two tempdir tests at the library seam (workspace's first): scaffold_writes_full_tree_from_embedded_assets (all files exist, all Written) + scaffold_output_matches_embedded_bytes_exactly (disk bytes == embedded bytes). Both use embedded_files() helper walking KB_ASSETS as independent source of truth.
- Module doc-comment documents the test pattern for follow-on tickets.

YAGNI: reset parameter, AssetId type, AssetStatus::Refreshed variant were dropped per AGENTS.md (defended in code-review); they land with the --reset ticket (fypvswcw).

Verification on merge: cargo test -p ws-kb 2/2 pass; cargo build -p ws-cli green; zero new warnings.

Unblocks ylr0ehfv (CLI), zqavq3i3 (idempotency), 9bpl9g4u (lint), d02zsd2d (SCHEMA content). Note for ylr0ehfv: scaffold signature is scaffold(root) (no reset param) — reconcile the stale 'scaffold(root, None)' in that ticket's description when implementing.

commit.message: feat(ws-kb): crate skeleton with scaffold() + tempdir test pattern

name: Wire ws kb init CLI subcommand + scaffold report
description:
## What to build

End-to-end: running ws kb init in a fresh directory scaffolds the complete knowledge-base tree from the binary and prints a human-readable report listing which assets were written/skipped. The latent bug (ws init not scaffolding catalog/knowledge/) is now closed at the standalone-command level. The CLI is a thin wrapper over ws-kb::scaffold.

## Implementation notes (from spec; reconciled after wdvr8o03)

- Add a Kb subcommand namespace to ws-cli: ws kb init (the --reset flag lands in ticket fypvswcw; this ticket delivers ws kb init with no-reset).
- Dispatch to ws-kb::scaffold(root). NOTE: the wdvr8o03 ticket dropped the reset parameter per YAGNI — the signature is scaffold(root) -> Result<ScaffoldReport>, NOT scaffold(root, None). The earlier 'scaffold(root, None)' in this description was stale.
- Print ScaffoldReport (written/skipped per asset) to stdout.
- Exit 0 on success; non-zero on filesystem error.
- Smoke test only at the CLI layer (the subcommand dispatches to the library without extra logic) — the library seam is tested in wdvr8o03.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] ws kb init dispatches to ws-kb::scaffold(root).
- [ ] Human-readable scaffold report (per-asset written/skipped) printed.
- [ ] Exit 0 on success; non-zero on FS error.
- [ ] Running ws kb init in a fresh tempdir produces a complete catalog/knowledge/ tree (closes latent bug at standalone-command level).
- [ ] Smoke test that the subcommand dispatches to the library.
result: Wired ws kb init CLI subcommand as a thin wrapper over ws-kb::scaffold(root). Dispatches to library, prints human-readable per-asset ScaffoldReport (written/skipped) to stdout, exits 0 on success / non-zero on FS error. Smoke test confirms subcommand dispatches to library. Merged via PR #4.

name: scaffold() idempotency: skip-by-default on an existing tree
description:
## What to build

End-to-end: re-running ws kb init (or scaffold()) on a workspace that already has catalog/knowledge/wiki/index.md leaves all existing wiki content untouched and fills only missing dirs/seed files. A workspace owner's accumulated wiki content is never destroyed on re-init.

## Implementation notes (from spec)

- On a populated root, scaffold creates only missing directories and missing seed files. Existing files are skipped (content unchanged).
- This makes ws kb init safe to re-run; --reset <asset> (ticket 4) is the explicit-refresh escape hatch.
- ScaffoldReport (ticket 1) must reflect skipped vs written status accurately — this ticket tightens that on the 'skip existing files' path.
- Follows the tempdir test pattern from ticket 1.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] scaffold on a pre-populated tree skips existing files (content unchanged, byte-for-byte).
- [ ] Only missing dirs and missing seed files are created.
- [ ] ScaffoldReport marks pre-existing assets as 'skipped', newly created ones as 'written'.
- [ ] Tempdir test: populate a tree with user-edited content, run scaffold, assert user content untouched and only gaps filled.
- [ ] cargo test -p ws-kb passes.
result: Implemented scaffold() skip-by-default idempotency. On a populated tree scaffold creates only missing dirs and seed files; existing files left byte-for-byte unchanged. ScaffoldReport reflects skipped vs written status accurately. Tempdir test with user-edited content confirms content untouched, only gaps filled. cargo test -p ws-kb green. Merged via PR #5.
